oneLiner: A native macOS terminal emulator (SwiftUI + embedded Ghostty) with a workspace model and CLI built for driving Claude Code sessions.
next: Validate harness v1.1.0 on a real multi-slice run: confirm async fan-out (`--no-wait` + `implement-wait.sh`) yields multi-branch overlap and that the STOP block/structured handoff flips the orchestrator output/resident inversion (ADR 0003); re-audit with session-audit.

milestones.title: Agent delegation (/tian implement) shipped in a release

shipped.summary: Released v0.18.0 (sidebar tab-name + per-tab branch rows): built, notarized (Apple Accepted), stapled, GitHub Release live, Sparkle appcast signed + pushed. Gotcha: the Sparkle EdDSA private key is NOT in the local keychain, so publish.sh dies at sign_update — signed the DMG directly via `sign_update --ed-key-file` after verifying the key derives to the app's SUPublicEDKey (KDEXWrfR…).

Sidebar per-tab rows now show the tab name (Row 1) with the git branch moved to Row 2 beside the diff/PR badges (arrow.triangle.branch icon + muted name); non-git tabs show the name only. Dropped the 3-row cap and the '+N more' overflow so every Claude-task tab gets a visible row. /orchestrate (single code-implementer per change, W=1), /code-review medium clean, Debug build green.

Sidebar per-tab worktree branch rows: each Claude-task tab shows its own branch + diff/PR badges (git status keyed by worktree root, not the shared --git-common-dir), a Claude->tian worktree bridge (`tian pane set-directory` + CwdChanged/PostToolUse hooks sync each pane's Claude cwd so EnterWorktree reflects in the sidebar), and an active-tab indicator. /code-review medium --fix landed 3 fixes: pin the active tab into the visible window, dedup the bridge on its own history so an OSC 7 write can't suppress it, and single-source the top-pane aggregation. Unit suite green.
